title: SwigAcyclic
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem swigAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) (hCADMG : G.IsCADMG)
    (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ⊆ G.V) :
    (G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW).IsAcyclic
-- claim_3_9 -- end statement
  := by
  -- TeX proof: tex/claim_3_9_proof_SwigAcyclic.tex
  -- Corollary derivation of sub-claim (a) from sub-claim (b) via
  -- `claim_3_2`'s `acyclic_iff_topological_order`, exactly as documented
  -- in the tex proof's "alternative derivation of (a) as a corollary of
  -- (b)" remark.  `hCADMG : G.IsCADMG` unfolds to `G.IsAcyclic`
  -- (`def_3_7`); apply `claim_3_2` `(⇒)` to obtain a topological order
  -- `lt` of `G`; lift to a topological order `splOrder lt` of the SWIG
  -- via `aux_swigTopologicalOrder`; conclude via `claim_3_2` `(⇐)`.
  -- Mirrors `splAcyclic`'s structure in `SplitTopologicalOrder.lean`.
  have hAcyclic : G.IsAcyclic := hCADMG
  obtain ⟨lt, hlt⟩ := (acyclic_iff_topological_order G).mp hAcyclic
  exact (acyclic_iff_topological_order (G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW)).mpr
    ⟨splOrder lt, aux_swigTopologicalOrder G hCADMG W hW lt hlt⟩

theorem swigTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (hCADMG : G.IsCADMG)
    (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ⊆ G.V)
    (lt : Node → Node → Prop) (hlt : G.IsTopologicalOrder lt) :
    (G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW).IsTopologicalOrder (splOrder lt)
-- claim_3_9 -- end statement
  := by
  -- TeX proof: tex/claim_3_9_proof_SwigAcyclic.tex
  -- One-line wrapper around the private workhorse
  -- `aux_swigTopologicalOrder`, which carries the full topological-order
  -- content (irreflexive / transitive / trichotomous on `J_{swig(W)} ∪
  -- V_{swig(W)}` plus parent-precedence on the SWIG edge set).  Mirrors
  -- `splTopologicalOrder`'s wrapper in `SplitTopologicalOrder.lean`.
  exact aux_swigTopologicalOrder G hCADMG W hW lt hlt
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem swigAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) (hCADMG : G.IsCADMG)
    (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ⊆ G.V) :
    (G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW).IsAcyclic

  := by

  have hAcyclic : G.IsAcyclic := hCADMG
  obtain ⟨lt, hlt⟩ := (acyclic_iff_topological_order G).mp hAcyclic
  exact (acyclic_iff_topological_order (G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW)).mpr
    ⟨splOrder lt, aux_swigTopologicalOrder G hCADMG W hW lt hlt⟩

theorem swigTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG Node) (hCADMG : G.IsCADMG)
    (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ⊆ G.V)
    (lt : Node → Node → Prop) (hlt : G.IsTopologicalOrder lt) :
    (G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW).IsTopologicalOrder (splOrder lt)

  := by

  exact aux_swigTopologicalOrder G hCADMG W hW lt hlt
lean_explanation:
This row formalises the LN remark `claim_3_9` as two separate theorems: `swigAcyclic` (sub-claim (a), acyclicity preservation) and `swigTopologicalOrder` (sub-claim (b), explicit topological-order construction). The implicit `Node : Type*` with `[DecidableEq Node]` is the ambient vertex type; the decidable-equality instance is needed because the SWIG construction returns a `CDMG (SplitNode Node)`, and `SplitNode Node` inherits `DecidableEq` from `Node` through the `deriving DecidableEq` clause on $\mathrm{def}\_3\_11$'s tagged-sum inductive (reused by $\mathrm{def}\_3\_12$).

The explicit arguments mirror the LN's data: `G : CDMG Node` is the ambient causal directed mixed graph (a 4-tuple $(J, V, E, L)$ per $\mathrm{def}\_3\_1$); `hCADMG : G.IsCADMG` is the CADMG attribute of $\mathrm{def}\_3\_7$ (which unfolds to `G.IsAcyclic` of $\mathrm{def}\_3\_6$); `W : Finset Node` is the LN's $W \subseteq V$ subset (encoded as a `Finset` to match `nodeSplittingHard`'s signature in $\mathrm{def}\_3\_12$); and `hW : W ⊆ G.V` is the LN's subset side-condition. For sub-claim (b), an additional pair `(lt : Node → Node → Prop, hlt : G.IsTopologicalOrder lt)` carries the LN's universal quantifier “for every topological order $<$ of $G$”.

The conclusion of `swigAcyclic` is `(G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW).IsAcyclic`: the SWIG (defined in $\mathrm{def}\_3\_12$) of $G$ with respect to $W$ satisfies $\mathrm{def}\_3\_6$'s `IsAcyclic` predicate — i.e.\ there is no non-trivial directed walk from any node to itself in $G_{\mathrm{swig}(W)}$. The conclusion of `swigTopologicalOrder` is `(G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW).IsTopologicalOrder (splOrder lt)`: the lex order `splOrder lt` on `SplitNode Node` — imported verbatim from `SplitTopologicalOrder.lean` — is a topological order of the SWIG in the sense of $\mathrm{def}\_3\_8$. Concretely, `splOrder lt` is a pure case-analysis lex order on the constructors of `SplitNode Node` with the orientation $\mathtt{.copy0} < \mathtt{.unsplit} < \mathtt{.copy1}$ per base node, which recovers the LN's index assignment $\phi(w^o) = j - 1/3 < j + 1/3 = \phi(w^i)$ without ever introducing rational arithmetic or an offset parameter. The `aux_swigTopologicalOrder` workhorse used in both proof bodies is a private helper that discharges the four `IsTopologicalOrder` obligations (irreflexive, transitive, trichotomous, parent-precedence) directly.
design_choices:
**Two separate theorems, not one bundled remark.** The LN packages (a) and (b) inside a single `\begin{Rem}`, but the two sub-claims have distinct downstream consumers: the do-calculus and counterfactual identification chapters typically need only sub-claim (a) to upgrade the SWIG's bare `CDMG (SplitNode Node)` return type to a CADMG, without ever mentioning a topological order. Bundling them into a single `swigAcyclic ∧ swigTopologicalOrder`-shaped theorem would force every such consumer to supply (or project past) a topological-order argument it never uses. The split mirrors `claim_3_6`'s `splAcyclic` / `splTopologicalOrder` choice and the chapter-3 pattern of one atomic predicate per sub-statement.

**Reuse `splOrder`, do not introduce a parallel `swigOrder`.** A reader of the LN might expect a fresh `swigOrder` aligned with the SWIG's $^o$ / $^i$ notation. We instead reuse `splOrder` — the lex order on `SplitNode Node` originally introduced for the node-splitting construction of $\mathrm{def}\_3\_11$ — because its semantics depend only on the constructor tag and the underlying `lt`, not on whether we are in the spl or SWIG construction. Concretely, the conventions $^0 \leftrightarrow \mathtt{.copy0}$ / $^o \leftrightarrow \mathtt{.copy0}$ (and similarly $^1$ / $^i \leftrightarrow \mathtt{.copy1}$) make the LN's index assignments coincide on the nose. Introducing a parallel `swigOrder := splOrder` would duplicate every irreflexivity / transitivity / trichotomy lemma already proven for `splOrder`, tempt future refactors to diverge them, and hide the structural identity that the same lex layout supports both constructions.

**`G.IsCADMG`, not `G.IsAcyclic`.** The LN explicitly reads “for a CADMG $G$”; $\mathrm{def}\_3\_7$'s `IsCADMG` is the named attribute and is also the hypothesis that `nodeSplittingHard` ($\mathrm{def}\_3\_12$) consumes in its own signature. Carrying `hCADMG : G.IsCADMG` lets the conclusion's `G.nodeSplittingHard hCADMG W hW` application thread through directly, without an `IsCADMG ↔ IsAcyclic` round-trip. LN-faithful naming wins over the micro-simplification of inlining the alias.

**`lt` typed as a bare `Node → Node → Prop`, not a `[LinearOrder Node]` typeclass.** The LN's “for any topological order $<$ of $G$” universally quantifies over an arbitrary binary relation that happens to satisfy `IsTopologicalOrder`; it does not presume that `Node` carries a canonical resolved order. Every well-foundedness / totality / parent-precedence property the proof body needs is carried by the explicit hypothesis `hlt : G.IsTopologicalOrder lt`, not by an ambient typeclass instance.

**`W : Finset Node`, not `Set Node`.** The SWIG construction builds the tagged-sum carrier $J \cup (V \setminus W) \sqcup W^o \sqcup W^i$ via `Finset.image`, `Finset.sdiff`, and `Finset` membership. Encoding `W` as a `Set Node` would force a `Finset → Set` coercion at every call site of `nodeSplittingHard`, with no LN-fidelity gain since the LN itself works with the finite set $W \subseteq V$.

**No transfer-edge sub-case in the parent-precedence proof.** Unlike `splTopologicalOrder`, which case-splits on lifted edges versus transfer edges $(\mathtt{.copy0}\, w, \mathtt{.copy1}\, w)$, the SWIG analogue has only the lifted-edge case: $\mathrm{def}\_3\_12$ item iii omits the transfer-edge set-builder entirely. This is exactly what makes the LN's $\phi(w^o) < \phi(w^i)$ choice consistent with the topological-order property — no edge of $E_{\mathrm{swig}(W)}$ runs $w^o \to w^i$ to constrain their relative order.
